title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Caucasus origin
description: Georgia (country) — 8,000-year-old qvevri wine vessels at Gadachrili Gora make this the oldest documented wine region on Earth (McGovern 2017).

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Caucasus origin
description: Armenia — Areni-1 cave winery ~4,100 BCE.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Greek + Roman Mediterranean spread
description: Athens — Greek wine culture: symposia, Dionysus, vase trade routes.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Greek + Roman Mediterranean spread
description: Rome — Roman viticulture spreads wine across entire empire; Falernian, Caecuban wines documented in Pliny.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Greek + Roman Mediterranean spread
description: Bordeaux France — Roman plantings ~1st century CE; medieval English-French wine trade makes Bordeaux the world's wine port.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Greek + Roman Mediterranean spread
description: Andalusia — Phoenician then Roman vineyards. Sherry (Jerez) trade to England anchors the modern fortified wine industry.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: New World wine transplant
description: Buenos Aires → Mendoza — Spanish missionaries bring grapevines 16th century. Argentina now #5 wine producer.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: New World wine transplant
description: Central Chile — phylloxera-free soils preserved pre-phylloxera European vines.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: New World wine transplant
description: Cape Town South Africa — 1659 Dutch settlers plant Groot Constantia; oldest New World wine region.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: New World wine transplant
description: Adelaide Australia — Barossa Valley, McLaren Vale. Largest Southern Hemisphere wine producer.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: New World wine transplant
description: Napa Valley California — 1976 Judgment of Paris blind tasting shocks the wine world.

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: wine word roots
description: Georgian — 'ghvino' — one of the oldest attested wine words.

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: wine word roots
description: Greek — 'oinos' (οἶνος). Parent of all Romance + Germanic wine words.

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: wine word roots
description: Latin — 'vinum'. Ancestor of vino/vin/vine/wine across 200+ modern languages.

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: wine word roots
description: French — 'vin'.

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: wine word roots
description: English — 'wine'. Germanic path but ultimately from Latin 'vinum'.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: Italy — world's #1 wine producer by volume (~50M hL/year).

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: France — #2 by volume, #1 by value (Bordeaux, Burgundy, Champagne).

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: Spain — #3 by volume, #1 by vineyard area.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: Napa / Sonoma — California is ~90% of US wine production.
